purpose: Define capability stages and measurable growth for architecture reasoning judgment.
load_condition: Load when coaching, improvement, or practice measurement is requested.

stages.name: Feature Implementer
stages.description: Implements features without assessing architectural impact. Does not zoom out. Treats boundary crossings as normal. Creates shallow modules. Does not distinguish local optimality from systemic impact.
indicators: Creates modules with many public methods
Says 'we'll refactor later' without scheduling it

stages.name: Boundary-Aware Developer
stages.description: Zooms out before implementing. Maps affected modules. Assesses boundary crossings. Creates deep modules. Distinguishes cross-cutting concerns from wrong boundaries. Records architectural decisions.
indicators: Assesses whether boundary crossings indicate wrong boundaries
Designs modules with small interfaces and hidden complexity

stages.name: Architecture Strategist
stages.description: Proactively identifies architectural decay before it manifests. Suggests boundary refactoring without being asked. Designs module structures that resist entropy. Makes architectural decisions that are proportional to the problem.
indicators: Identifies decay before it becomes a problem
Designs module structures that minimize future coupling
Makes proportional architectural decisions — neither over-engineered nor under-designed

evolution_layers.name: From feature implementer to boundary-aware
evolution_layers.capability: Consistently zoom out and assess architectural impact before implementing

evolution_layers.name: From boundary-aware to strategist
evolution_layers.capability: Proactively identify and prevent architectural decay

measurement.how: Count the number of changes that cross module boundaries without explicit assessment, divided by total changes.
Average ratio of functionality provided to interface surface across all modules. Higher is better.
measurement.threshold: ST-002: >2:1 (2 units of functionality per interface point). ST-003: >3:1.

measurement.how: Count the number of modules that have accumulated shallow additions (new public methods that expose internals) without boundary refactoring, over time.
measurement.threshold: ST-002: <2 shallow additions per month. ST-003: 0 (immediate refactoring when detected).
